channel.title: Ramah: Good News Feed
channel.description: Positive news stories from around the world, filtered by sentiment analysis

item.title: Why aren’t Republicans thrilled by the fall in teen pregnancies? | Arwa Mahdawi
item.description: Teenagers these days, eh? Instead of having unprotected sex and popping out babies, they’re wasting their time on TikTok, or something.

item.title: US edges closer to popular vote deciding winner of presidential elections
item.description: A national majority vote for president is one step closer to reality after the Virginia governor, Abigail Spanberger, signed the national popular vote bill into law, joining an interstate compact with 17 other states and the District of Columbia.

item.title: UFOs and a Tardis - why unusual holiday stays are booming
item.description: In a secluded corner of Pembrokeshire, a structure resembling a landed spacecraft rises unexpectedly from the landscape.

item.title: 'Welcome home': Watch joyful reunion as Orion capsule opened after splashdown
item.description: Newly released video shows the moment a recovery crew opens the hatch of Artemis II's Orion capsule to a joyful reunion with the four astronauts inside.

item.title: Carney says it’s Canada’s ‘time to come together’ after Liberals secure majority
item.description: Mark Carney has said he will govern with “humility, determination and a clear understanding of what this moment demands” after his Liberals swept three byelections Monday evening, forging a parliamentary majority just more than a year after he took power.

item.title: Politicians and media can’t imagine a fossil-fuel free world - and it’s holding Australians back from huge EV savings | Adam Morton
item.description: It is tempting to think about what could have been.

item.title: Zelenskyy hails Magyar’s win over Orbán as ‘the victory of light over darkness’ in Hungary – as it happened
item.description: Ukraine’s president Volodymyr Zelenskyy has hailed the opposition win in Hungary as “the victory of light over darkness,” as he called for “pragmatic, friendly” relations with the new administration.

item.title: Carney wins majority government as Canadian by-elections boost Liberals
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney has secured a majority government after winning three by-elections on Monday.

item.title: Scottish Greens pledge free bus travel and basic income in election manifesto
item.description: The Scottish Greens have called for free bus travel, thousands of extra teachers and doctors and a universal basic income among hundreds of uncosted manifesto pledges.

item.title: Super Typhoon Sinlaku pounds remote US islands in the Pacific Ocean with ferocious winds
item.description: A super typhoon is taking aim at several remote U.S.

item.title: Millions of people are pretending to be AI chatbots — for fun
item.description: The website Your AI Slop Bores Me takes its name from a meme people on social media use to criticize AI-generated content.

item.title: Virginia joins a national effort to ensure only popular vote winners become president
item.description: A national effort to circumvent the Electoral College has gained another state.

item.title: Households could get free electricity for doing washing on sunny weekends
item.description: Households and businesses could get free or cheaper electricity from their energy company for running appliances during periods of excess supply, such as sunny weekends.

item.title: Chalamet thanked by Royal Ballet and Opera boss for boosting ticket sales
item.description: The head of the Royal Ballet and Opera (RBO) has thanked Hollywood star Timothée Chalamet for boosting the London institution's ticket sales following the actor's controversial remarks about the art forms.

item.title: ‘It was life-changing’: the celebrated art historian who spent 46 years sitting for Frank Auerbach
item.description: Last November, a work titled Potiphar’s Wife by British painter Euan Uglow appeared in a private sale by Christie’s in London.

item.title: ‘They accomplished so much, even as they were dying’: the groundbreaking gay art of Peter Hujar and Paul Thek
item.description: Andrew Durbin, author and editor-in-chief of Frieze Magazine, spent almost five years writing The Wonderful World That Almost Was.

item.title: Take heart, Keir Starmer – Australia’s PM faced similarly dim prospects, and he triumphed | Tim Soutphommasane
item.description: It has never been worse for Labour, at least according to the polls.

item.title: Ben Stokes plays down talk of McCullum disagreement but plans ‘different’ path to success
item.description: Ben Stokes has moved to play down suggestions of a disagreement between himself and Brendon McCullum, insisting he and the England head coach remain aligned despite an Ashes defeat that, at times, suggested otherwise.

item.title: Ukraine hopes for more cooperation after Hungary elects new prime minister
item.description: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.

item.title: Rescuers free young whale after 24-hour stranding ordeal
item.description: A whale stranded on a sandbar in Wallis Lake on the NSW mid north coast has been refloated by marine animal rescue teams.

item.title: Asian stocks gain and oil falls on hopes of renewed US-Iran talks
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Asian stocks were trading higher, tracking Wall Street gains, and oil fell on Tuesday as expectations rose over a possible second round of talks between the U.S.

item.title: Carney secures Liberal majority after special election wins
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberal Party has secured a slim majority in the House of Commons, solidifying its hold on power after being projected by Canadian media to win at least two seats in by-elections on Monday.

item.title: Mark Carney secures majority government in Canada after special election win
item.description: Canadian prime minister Mark Carney has secured a parliamentary majority for his Liberal government, CBC News reported.

item.title: Quantum computing: A tech race Europe could win?
item.description: In a lab on the western edge of Paris, where the River Seine flows wide and trams slide past glass-fronted buildings and blossoming cherry trees, a technician called Rémi makes some adjustments with a spanner.

item.title: Lebanon seeks peace, but Hezbollah needs to be convinced first
item.description: With Lebanon, again, engulfed by war, I remember a meeting I had with President Joseph Aoun at the Baabda Palace, a modernist building at the top of a hill overlooking Beirut last August.

item.title: Doctors' strikes can have surprising benefits - but are they sustainable?
item.description: That was how one hospital boss recalled hearing news of a walkout by resident doctors in England last December.

item.title: NSW promises more fast chargers and electric trucks in revamped EV policy
item.description: Electric truck development and building more stations to charge them will be core pillars of a state’s revamped electric vehicle strategy designed to ease the pressure of rising fuel costs.

item.title: Ukulele club's 600 members may be strumming their way to better brain health
item.description: It's mid-week and mid-morning at this Queensland RSL club — but it might as well be a Saturday night.

item.title: Why Gout's incredible run deserves equal measures of praise and patience
item.description: Gout Gout's extraordinary Australian 200 metre record in Sydney did more than rewrite the record books.

item.title: Retro Rewind re-creates the glorious drudgery of working a '90s video store
item.description: What the nostalgic throwback lacks in complexity it makes up for in repetitive charm.

item.title: Manchester United 1-2 Leeds United: Premier League – live reaction
item.description: double quotation markIt’s a massive result for us.

item.title: Arne Slot backs Liverpool to produce ‘great performance’ to beat PSG
item.description: Arne Slot has said Liverpool do not face an impossible task against Paris Saint-Germain but must produce the perfect performance to overcome the European champions in the quarter-finals of the Champions League.

item.title: Former NFL cornerback Chris Payton-Jones dies at 30: ‘Everyone loved him’
item.description: Former NFL cornerback Chris Payton-Jones died in a car accident Saturday night in Florida.

item.title: Renewed ties with EU needed to boost UK security and economy, says Starmer
item.description: The economic and security benefits of a closer relationship with the EU are “simply too big to ignore”, Keir Starmer has told parliament as the British government prepares for more rapid alignment with European rules.

item.title: Péter Magyar vows to pursue those who ‘plundered’ Hungary, after election win
item.description: Hungary’s prime minister-elect, Péter Magyar, has pledged to pursue those who “plundered, looted, betrayed, indebted and ruined” his country, promising “a new era” after a landslide election victory over his far-right predecessor Viktor Orbán.

item.title: Should we be using cotton buds to clean our ears?
item.description: Greg Foot asks whether we should be using cotton buds to clean our ears.

item.title: Hungarian election winner Magyar vows to rebuild EU relationship after stunning defeat of Viktor Orbán - Europe live
item.description: After some 20 minutes, Magyar is turning towards foreign policy now.

item.title: Sistren review – a profound love story between two best friends (told with memes, lip-syncs and confetti)
item.description: It’s only April, but the most profound love story on a Sydney stage this year may just be the soulmate-friendship between Isla and Violet in Sistren.

item.title: Spring flowers bloom in England after heatwave and cold snap create ideal conditions
item.description: Colourful tulips, hyacinths, wisteria and daffodils have made a remarkable display across England after a short period of hot weather followed by a cold snap created excellent conditions for spring blooms.

item.title: Not over, but done: Lamine Yamal all smiles as he guides Barcelona to verge of title
item.description: Lamine Yamal had not crossed the line yet but he was celebrating already, everyone else following him.

item.title: Bollywood classics, rave bangers and Michael Stipe duets: 10 of Asha Bhosle’s greatest recordings
item.description: With more than 12,000 songs to her name, Indian playback singer Asha Bhosle is one of the most recorded and well-known voices in Bollywood cinema.

item.title: Rukmini Iyer’s quick and easy recipe for chilli eggs with miso beans and spinach | Quick and easy
item.description: My go-to cheat ingredient for a dash of heat is White Mausu’s peanut rāyu – it has a gentler flavour profile than, say, Lao Gan Ma crispy chilli in oil, and works perfectly in this dish of creamy, lemon-spiked beans and eggs.

item.title: Celebrations in McIlroy's home town at history-maker's back-to-back Masters wins
item.description: Rory McIlroy knows how to put his fans through the ringer - and Sunday at Augusta National was no different.

item.title: Social media was once a great global conversation. Now it’s just individuals locked into their own private worlds | Tom Whyman
item.description: I used to post an awful lot on Twitter.

item.title: Can red light therapy really deliver a beauty and health glow up? Here's the science
item.description: Red light therapy has become the latest ray of hope in the wellness industry.

item.title: New report suggests Democrats have better odds in some upcoming Senate races
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks Jessica Taylor of the Cook Political Report about a new report on the midterm outlook for U.S.

item.title: Weather tracker: Super Typhoon Sinlaku threatens Mariana Islands
item.description: The Mariana Islands archipelago in the western Pacific, home to the US territories of Guam and the Northern Mariana Islands, are bracing for extreme weather early this week as Super Typhoon Sinlaku approaches.

item.title: ‘So beautiful’: Rachel Zegler hails accessibility of Evita’s free balcony scene
item.description: It was the most talked about theatre scene of the year: Rachel Zegler performing Don’t Cry for Me Argentina from the balcony at the London Palladium to crowds gathered on the street below.

item.title: European leaders celebrate Péter Magyar’s victory in a stunning Hungarian election
item.description: Hungarian voters have ousted long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán after 16 years in power, rejecting the authoritarian policies and global far-right movement that he embodied in favor of a pro-European challenger in a bombshell election result with global repercussions.

item.title: Can you solve it? Are you smarter than a Navy admiral?
item.description: Tanya has now written her first book, Mathematical Puzzles and Curiosities, in collaboration with two other puzzle enthusiasts, Ivo David and Yogev Shpilman.

item.title: My search for the perfect bodega in Madrid
item.description: The first hurdle to overcome when searching for the Spanish capital’s top bodegas is the correct interpretation of the word “bodega”.

item.title: Rory McIlroy wins the Masters for second year in a row
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga.

item.title: 'School breakfast club has best toast in the world'
item.description: A primary school says attendance has improved and pupils are "more settled" since a free breakfast club was set up.

item.title: 'I should have been safe on a pavement' - woman's injuries show reality of being hit by an e-bike
item.description: Sandy Peters doesn't remember anything at all about the crash itself.

item.title: Borlase leads host of Opals stars to sign new WNBA free agency deals
item.description: WNBL MVP Izzy Borlase has signed a new deal with the Atlanta Dream in the WNBA free agency period.

item.title: €1m Picasso painting to be won for €100 in charity raffle
item.description: A raffle in France is offering the chance to win a portrait by Pablo Picasso for the price of a €100 (£87) ticket, with proceeds going to Alzheimer’s research.

item.title: Sit, stay, slay: Anton the poodle named best in show at Sydney’s Royal Easter Show
item.description: Anton knew he’d won, jumping up and down as though he’d crossed a finish line first, celebrating as though someone had left a gate open.

item.title: ‘Hungary has chosen Europe’: EU leaders jubilant after Péter Magyar’s victory over Orbán
item.description: EU leaders heaped praise on Péter Magyar after his decisive election victory in Hungary against the long-serving prime minister Viktor Orbán, who many saw as a direct threat to Europe’s peace and prosperity.

item.title: Loved ones fight for change after melanoma takes another young life
item.description: Emily Robertson-Miller, 29, died from melanoma, after the skin cancer metastasised and spread to her brain.

item.title: Day rues missed opportunities after golden chance at the Masters
item.description: Australia's Jason Day finished tied for 12th at The Masters after being fifth heading into the final round.

item.title: 'A truly historic moment': Hungarian opposition wins election landslide
item.description: Prime Minister Viktor Orbán has conceded defeat in the Hungarian election after 16 years in power, with the opposition securing a landslide election win.

item.title: Children and 75yo among dozens of homeless in wealthy holiday town
item.description: More that 40 people have been found to be homeless in Margaret River, including a 75-year-old.

item.title: The surprising reality of how teenage girls still define themselves
item.description: A felt‑tip sign taped to the door of a private room announces "GIRLS ONLY", "Boy's don't Eneter!" [sic], and, by way of a cheeky flourish, "don't worry boys!".

item.title: An Aussie rugby win at home is one thing but victory in NZ is another
item.description: The Reds' 31-26 win over the Crusaders was not pretty, but substance is more important than style when you beat the defending Super Rugby Pacific champions.

item.title: Hungary's Orbán era ends in a landslide election win for opposition
item.description: Centre-right Tisza Party leader Péter Magyar is on course to become Hungary's new prime minister with a two-thirds majority.

item.title: Loved, but costly — is the end near for this heritage railway?
item.description: The Tasmanian government is hosting meetings about the future of the West Coast Wilderness Railway, which is a significant employer and economic driver for Queenstown and Strahan.

item.title: Wout van Aert outduels Tadej Pogacar to win his first Paris-Roubaix title
item.description: Wout van Aert shattered a decade-old jinx to win Paris-Roubaix on Sunday, outduelling the world champion, Tadej Pogacar, in a brutal classic race across the cobbles, while Franziska Koch edged out Marianne Vos in the women’s race.

item.title: Australia's best inventor you've never heard of
item.description: The rise of solar power shows no sign of slowing down, with the International Energy Agency predicting it will overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year.

item.title: How recycling can help solve a 'huge issue' in tennis
item.description: A new recycling initiative in Melbourne is turning used tennis balls into footwear.

item.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Nasa's Artemis crew welcomed home
item.description: The four astronauts who flew in Nasa's Artemis II mission around the moon have been welcomed home.

item.title: Artemis II crew speak out at welcome home event: ‘Earth was this lifeboat hanging in the universe’
item.description: Still marveling over their moon mission, the Artemis II astronauts received a thunderous welcome home on Saturday from the hundreds of colleagues who took part in setting a record for deep space travel during the US space agency Nasa’s lunar comeback.

item.title: Socialising, work, exercise: what makes a good day and is there a ‘formula’ for making it better?
item.description: What edges a day over the threshold from being humdrum to something better? Something good?.

item.title: Saturday Night Live: Colman Domingo makes for season’s best host to date
item.description: Moving on to more pressing business, Trump rings up Tiger Woods (Kenan Thompson), currently in rehab in Switzerland after flipping his car and getting a DUI but hangs up on him when informed there’s no golf course on site.

item.title: ‘It feels as if I’ve made a new best friend’: my experiment with AI journalling
item.description: Ever since I was a teenager, I have kept some form of diary.

item.title: Charlotte Riley: ‘The girl in the photo would be thrilled I’m an actor’
item.description: Born in Grindon, County Durham, in 1981, Charlotte Riley is an actor and writer.

item.title: Remote working tribunal cases in Great Britain fall for first time since Covid
item.description: The number of workers in Great Britain taking their bosses to employment tribunals over remote working fell last year for the first time since Covid hit, with a tightening labour market making some more reluctant to leave roles despite return-to-office mandates.

item.title: Is AI the greatest art heist in history?
item.description: In 2026, its easy to see why generative AI is bad.

item.title: Hull closes national titles on her terms with 5k win after shock 1,500m fall
item.description: After a disastrous 1,500 metres final where she fell and finished last, Olympic silver medallist Jess Hull considered pulling the pin on the Australian Athletics Championships in Sydney.

item.title: This is how we do it: ‘I love the idea of only knowing one person intimately for the rest of my life’
item.description: Studying on different continents is a challenge for Veronika and Fabio … Can their young love go the distance?.

item.title: Wallaroos’ hopes of breakthrough win washed away by Canada in stormy conditions
item.description: The Wallaroos have fallen short in their quest for a breakthrough victory over Canada, but can take heart from a 24-0 defeat in their opening match of the Pacific Four rugby series.

item.title: Hopes more pine martens will be born in the wild
item.description: Conservationists say pine martens are "doing well" after being being reintroduced to parts of south-west England.

item.title: Are some things better left unsaid, even if you're getting married?
item.description: It's safe to say Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new film The Drama has been starting a lot of conversations.

item.title: Hiker found 'safe and well' after spending 24 hours lost in Victoria's north-west
item.description: Andy, 25, went missing after separating from his group to do a solo hike in the Murray-Sunset National Park on Saturday morning.

item.title: Love story: 'It's not my kidney now, it's yours. I'm just holding it for you'
item.description: Despite a 10-year age gap, brothers Sajarn and Codey Stow, who share Juru, Bindal and Kaantju cultural heritage, have shared a close bond since their childhood in north Queensland.

item.title: 'Special thing to be a human': Artemis II crew welcomed home after moon mission
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: Debutant scores as Matildas kick off FIFA Series with thumping win
item.description: The Matildas have cruised to a 5-0 win over Malawi to kick off their FIFA Series in Kenya.

item.title: WA firefighter encouraging more women to join front line
item.description: Volunteer firefighter Kahlia Lloyd wants to hold an annual day encouraging women to try firefighting in Western Australia's south-west.

item.title: Hungarians decide whether to end 16 years of Orbán rule and elect rival
item.description: Hungarians go to the polls on Sunday in a vote that could bring down long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán and have significant repercussions for the rest of Europe, the US and Russia.

item.title: Excitement, tears and relief: How it felt to follow Nasa's historic mission to the Moon
item.description: For the last 10 days, four astronauts have been making history, travelling further into space than humans have been before as they voyaged to the Moon and back.

item.title: McIlroy knows he has to be better to win Masters after big lead disappears
item.description: Rory McIlroy shares the lead with Cameron Young on 11-under-par after the third round of the Masters, with Australian Jason Day just three strokes back.

item.title: Nick Xenophon-founded SA Best party comes to an end
item.description: SA Best was formed by Nick Xenophon in 2017 ahead of the 2018 state election.

item.title: 'It's a special thing to be on Planet Earth': Artemis crew welcomed home in Houston
item.description: The four astronauts who flew in Nasa's Artemis II mission around the Moon have been welcomed back to Planet Earth at an event in Houston, Texas.

item.title: Brian Cox: ‘We don’t know how powerful AI is going to become – it’s both exciting and potentially a problem’
item.description: What is the inspiration behind your latest live show, Emergence?.

item.title: A budget 'leak' so good, the journalist's editors didn't believe it
item.description: With so many terrible stories in the news at the moment, do you want to hear a light-hearted tale?.

item.title: Rio Ngumoha sparks Liverpool win over wasteful Fulham with first Anfield goal
item.description: At the end of a stormy week for Arne Slot there was respite in the form of a morale-boosting display from Rio Ngumoha.

item.title: Still trying to prove you're good at trivia? You're not fooling me
item.description: I applaud your courage for having a go at this quiz.

item.title: Masters winner is set to make $4.5 million out of a majors-record $22.5 million prize purse
item.description: AUGUSTA, Ga.

item.title: Amy Cokayne powers England to opening Six Nations win against Ireland
item.description: The Women’s Six Nations is still searching for a giantkiller, with the ever-dominant England not needing a consistent performance to overcome one of their main challengers in Ireland.

item.title: I Am Maximus joins Grand National greats by regaining crown to emulate Red Rum
item.description: In a race where fortunes can switch in an instant, I Am Maximus and his trainer, Willie Mullins, are on a roll like few others in Grand National history.

item.title: Australia v Malawi: Fifa Series women’s international football friendly – live
item.description: OK Kerr is off and Remy Siemsen is coming on.

item.title: The Great Green Wall's one of the world's most ambitious eco-projects. Is it working?
item.description: In an arid valley near the village of Kourtimale in southern Djibouti, a tattered chain link fence marks the boundaries of what was once Abdi Guelleh's farm.

item.title: Welcome To Wrexham streaming series renewed for three more seasons
item.description: Hit streaming series Welcome To Wrexham, which has been charting the revival of the city's football team, has been renewed for a further three seasons.

item.title: World's first safari park celebrates 60 years
item.description: A long-serving worker has shared his memories and favourite animals at a safari park he calls his "first home" as it celebrates 60 years of being open to the public.

item.title: ‘This cactus looks as if it’s preaching’: Joseph Cyr’s best phone picture
item.description: It was a school holiday, so Cyr was doing a trail run when he took this image.

item.title: Djibouti's president wins unprecedented sixth term with 97.8% of vote
item.description: Djibouti's President Ismail Omar Guelleh has won an election with 97.8% of the vote, keeping him at the helm of the strategically-placed Horn of Africa nation for a sixth term, preliminary official results show.

item.title: Welcome to the fairytale land of national treasures – the Stephen Collins cartoon
item.description: Continue reading.

item.title: How AI is getting better at finding security holes
item.description: In the past few months, AI models have gone from producing hallucinations to becoming effective at finding security flaws in software, according to developers who maintain widely used cyber infrastructure.

item.title: Artemis crew home safely after completing historic mission to the Moon
item.description: The four astronauts who flew in Nasa's Artemis II mission around the Moon have splashed down safely in the Pacific Ocean after a flawless return.

item.title: How NASA and the Artemis II crew completed the 'perfect' splashdown
item.description: Nine days, one hour, 32 minutes and 15 seconds.

item.title: The hill I will die on: Yes, money can buy you happiness – if you spend it right | Eleanor Margolis
item.description: When wages have stalled for nearly 20 years and I recently came face to face with a tube of toothpaste that was nearly £7 in my local Sainsbury’s, the idea that money can’t buy happiness seems almost offensive.

item.title: Tyson Fury’s latest return unlikely to save heavyweight era reaching its end
item.description: So here we are, back watching Fury prepare to climb through the ropes to fight Arslanbek Makhmudov, the giant Russian from Dagestan, at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium on Saturday night.

item.title: Live: US and Iranian delegations set for crucial peace talks
item.description: US Vice-President JD Vance and the US delegation has arrived in Islamabad, Pakistan to enter into peace talks with Iran.

item.title: Hollingsworth sensationally reinstated as national champion after initial DQ
item.description: Claudia Hollingsworth has been reinstated as the women's 1,500m winner at the Australian Athletics Championships in Sydney.

item.title: Meet the kids hoping to become European cup-stacking champions
item.description: If the answer is "no" then the European Sport Stacking Championships are probably not for you.

item.title: ‘TikTok effect’ brings sellout crowds and younger fans to Grand National meeting
item.description: Everywhere you looked that sentiment was being echoed and magnified by 55,000 other racegoers, most dressed in their finest suits and silks, who were basking in the first Ladies’ Day sellout since 2012.

item.title: American Classic review – I defy you not to fall in love with Kevin Kline and Laura Linney’s tender comedy
item.description: Ah, the roar of the greasepaint – the smell of the crowd! Who doesn’t love the theatre? Or at least the idea of the theatre.

item.title: Heathers the Musical review – dark teen comedy is hammy fun, if thoroughly defanged
item.description: Before Mean Girls, there was Heathers.

item.title: Anisa Nandaula: No Small Talk review – chatty fun with rising star of Australian comedy
item.description: If you came to Anisa Nandaula’s new show, No Small Talk, expecting to be safe from audience interaction, prepare to be surprised.

item.title: Live: Swans take care of Suns at Norwood before Hawks-Bulldogs blockbuster
item.description: Sydney dominates a top-four clash with Gold Coast at suburban Norwood Oval to take top spot on the ladder for now, ahead of a Hawthorn - Western Bulldogs blockbuster.

item.title: This rich, meat-free curry is a budget-friendly way to make a massaman
item.description: Hetty Lui McKinnon is a food writer and cookbook author with a passion for vegetables.

item.title: An inappropriate joke nearly ended his career. Now he's back with more humour
item.description: In an Indian city, an unassuming, slightly rumpled comedian walks into the spotlight for the first time in months.

item.title: Artemis II astronauts emerge from capsule after safely returning to Earth
item.description: SBS acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of Country and their connections and continuous care for the skies, lands and waterways throughout Australia.

item.title: McIlroy's 'just keep swinging' approach takes him to huge Masters lead
item.description: A stunning conclusion to the second round has Rory McIlroy well clear at the Masters, with Jason Day slipping on the final hole.

item.title: Rory McIlroy surges into six-shot Masters lead with stunning second-round flourish
item.description: After spending 16 years as the hunter, Rory McIlroy is relishing his position as the hunted.

item.title: Teen shuns screens to turn love of chickens into growing egg business
item.description: Tamworth teenager Max Lye might be the one to answer the age-old question: what came first, the chicken or the egg?.

item.title: Live: Astronauts in 'excellent shape' after a 'perfect' descent in Pacific Ocean
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, surpassing the record set by Apollo 13 in 1970.

item.title: Fresh push for Murray Basin freight rail to get more produce off trucks
item.description: Farmers are demanding the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project to get more freight on rail and off roads.

item.title: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he set up in Africa to honor his mother Princess Diana
item.description: LONDON (AP) — A charity co-founded by Prince Harry in Africa to honor his late mother, Princess Diana, has sued him for defamation after he stepped down as a patron last year.

item.title: Great at gaming? US air traffic control wants you to apply
item.description: People adept at video games should consider taking jobs as air traffic controllers, the US government has said, as it tries to address a shortage of workers in the sector.

item.title: Artemis II’s grand moon finale is almost here with a Pacific splashdown to cap NASA’s lunar comeback
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Their dramatic grand finale fast approaching, Artemis II’s astronauts aimed for a splashdown in the Pacific on Friday to close out humanity’s first voyage to the moon in more than a half-century.

item.title: Remaining in Nato is in best interests of US, says Keir Starmer
item.description: Keir Starmer has said it is in the best interests of the US to stay in Nato and that Europe must do more to support the alliance in light of the war in Iran.

item.title: Shortlisted for an Oscar, 'Homebound' is a daring movie about two dear friends
item.description: That's because Neeraj Ghaywan, director of Homebound, didn't want to go public with his movie until it was ready.

item.title: Mandelson faces £300 fine for urinating in street
item.description: Lord Peter Mandelson is set to be fined up to £300 for public urination in London, however Kensington and Chelsea council is unable to find a suitable address to send the fixed penalty notice to.

item.title: Why weight-loss jabs work better for some people than others
item.description: People who carry variations in two genes linked to appetite and digestion can lose more weight when taking drugs such as Wegovy and Mounjaro to treat obesity, research suggests.

item.title: Sun shines on Grand National Ladies Day
item.description: This year's annual Ladies Day at Aintree Racecourse is under way, with the biggest crowd in almost 15 years expected.

item.title: Drug dealer busted after sending picture of new 'Turkey teeth' to friend
item.description: A drug dealer who was caught after sending a photo of his "Turkey teeth" to a friend has been jailed for four years.

item.title: Is a 'Super El Niño' on the way and how will it affect the UK?
item.description: Climate scientists are predicting a change in weather patterns around the globe later this year due to an expected El Niño event.

item.title: Super Mario what?! The seven best obscure Mario games
item.description: As The Super Mario Galaxy Movie storms the box office, we look back at the best forgotten games inspired by Tetris, Lemmings and … vitamins?.

item.title: Overwhelmed by kid clutter? Get organized with these 7 smart tips
item.description: A million magnetic tiles scattered across the living room.

item.title: More ambulances are carrying blood for transfusions. Experts say it will save lives
item.description: More and more ambulances carry blood when responding to trauma scenes.

item.title: Live: Cowboys claim thrilling Queensland derby victory with last-minute try
item.description: North Queensland Cowboys have won a thriller at Lang Park, beating the Brisbane Broncos 35-31.

item.title: Thanks to Artemis II humanity has returned to the moon in person! Is this good? Nobody can say | First Dog on the Moon
item.description: It is actually a bit shit on the moon (THE MOON)Sign up here to get an email whenever First Dog cartoons are publishedGet all your needs met at the First Dog shop if what you need is First Dog merchandise and prints Continue reading.

item.title: A rare sign of hope as mountain gorillas welcome two sets of twins in Africa
item.description: I will never forget the moment I first saw a mountain gorilla.

item.title: Go Gentle by Maria Semple review – a joyfully clever New York romcom
item.description: What would Marcus Aurelius have made of the Kardashians? Would Seneca have been amused by mindfulness apps? These were questions I had never consciously pondered before reading Maria Semple’s new novel.

item.title: Asian stocks mostly higher and oil gains ahead of planned U.S.-Iran peace talks
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Asian stocks were mostly up Friday tracking Wall Street gains while oil prices also rose on the fragile Iran war ceasefire and ahead of Iran-U.S.

item.title: Australia’s Talia Gibson stunned by Great Britain teenager Mika Stojsavljevic in BJK Cup opener
item.description: Australia’s quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup finals is off to the worst possible start with spearhead Talia Gibson a shock loser to British teen Mika Stojsavljevic.

item.title: Reich: The Sextets album review – Colin Currie celebrates the minimalist master’s joy of six
item.description: The Colin Currie Group formed 20 years ago to honour Steve Reich’s 70th birthday with a performance of Drumming.

item.title: Six-year-old who called 999 to save mum hailed a hero
item.description: A six-year-old girl who called 999 and saved her mother's life when she suffered a bleed on the brain has been honoured, along with her younger sister, by London Ambulance Service (LAS).

item.title: Benjamina Ebuehi’s sweet and salty chocolate chip cookies recipe | The sweet spot
item.description: Everyone has different ideas on what makes the perfect chocolate chip cookie, with everything from thickness and chewiness to the amount of chocolate up for debate.

item.title: Snoozing seal blocks road in popular coastal spot
item.description: A sleeping seal has held up motorists making their way along the Mornington Peninsula, causing a major road to be closed in both directions in the popular coastal area after it stopped to rest on the roadway.

item.title: Artemis II crew on inspiring the next generation and unifying humanity – video
item.description: As the Artemis II mission enters flight day nine, the crew spoke about inspiring the next generation and 'working on something big for the good of everyone' ahead of their return to earth.

item.title: Bronwyn Kuss: Bronwyn & Sons review – parchingly dry comedy about success in your late 30s
item.description: A drunk witch once told Bronwyn Kuss to have a baby.

item.title: Euphoria ushered in a new generation of A-listers. Now, it's finally back
item.description: Welcome to Cheat Sheet, where we give you all the intel you need about iconic shows your friends have been bugging you to watch! In honour of its long awaited return after four years, we're diving into teen (and now adult) drama, Euphoria.

item.title: Artemis II crew to return home as Nasa lays out steps for safe splashdown
item.description: The crew of Artemis II is set to return to earth on Friday following its historic 10-day lunar flyby mission, and Nasa leaders have described the precise logistics needed to get them home.

item.title: US agency proposes rolling back rules for safe disposal of toxic coal ash
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency on Thursday proposed weakened rules governing the safe disposal of ash produced by burning coal.

item.title: Super members blindsided by change in fund terms and conditions
item.description: TelstraSuper (which operates independently of the telecommunication provider) has closed down its Direct Access investment product without properly notifying members.

item.title: How one of the world's best ballet dancers ended up in Australia
item.description: When I asked ballet dancer David Hallberg during an interview a few years ago to name the one thing people wouldn't know about him, the man described as the 'Nureyev of his generation' gave a secret smile.

item.title: Whole lotta love for Led Zeppelin star given plaque
item.description: Led Zeppelin superstar Robert Plant left shoppers dazed and confused when he was spotted crate digging in the world's oldest record store.

item.title: Anna Wintour’s Vogue cover is more than a cameo – it’s a power play
item.description: In the world of magazines, when someone announces they’re leaving a job, their colleagues will traditionally present them with their own personalised mock-up of the magazine’s front cover.

item.title: Is space exploration worth the money and effort? | Letters
item.description: Zoe Williams speaks for many of us when she notes that the US space mission is pointless (Let’s stop going into space.

item.title: I took up the ukulele after a chance celebrity encounter. It’s brought me more happiness than falling in love | Melanie Tait
item.description: Avowed ukulele lover George Harrison once wrote in a note to a friend “Everyone I know who is into the ukulele is ‘crackers’.

item.title: Gout and Kennedy renew rivalry, Hull eyes history as Australian athletics puts its best on show
item.description: An array of exotic, well-trimmed dogs will parade around Sydney Olympic Park this weekend as part of the Royal Easter Show.

item.title: Canada's Mark Carney 'so proud' of astronauts in call to Artemis II
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney told the astronauts of the Artemis II mission that Canadians 'couldn't be more proud' of them.

item.title: The best water flossers in the UK, tested for that dentist-clean feeling
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: The Housemaid author Freida McFadden reveals her true identity
item.description: The bestselling thriller writer known as Freida McFadden has revealed her real identity, ending years of speculation about the author behind a string of hugely popular novels.

item.title: Britain's 'most beautiful coin' sold for £110,000
item.description: A coin considered the "most beautiful" ever minted in Britain has sold at auction for £110,000.

item.title: Dream-pop at its most divine: Cocteau Twins’ 20 greatest songs – ranked!
item.description: At first, Cocteau Twins gave every impression of being a goth band: check out Wax and Wane’s Banshees-esque ambience – the guitar is very John McGeoch – flanged bass and drum machine.

item.title: Most Hungarians want better relations with EU after years of Orbán Brussels-bashing, poll finds
item.description: Hungary elections: what is at stake and who is likely to win?.

item.title: My New Band Believe review – beautiful ideas burst from ex-Black Midi man’s lovable debut album
item.description: In the middle of Hellfire, the final album by British art-rockers Black Midi, lurked a song called Still.

item.title: Belle and Sebastian review – joyful anniversary tour makes debut album brighter than ever
item.description: It’s a double 30th anniversary for Belle and Sebastian, whose first two albums, Tigermilk and If You’re Feeling Sinister, both came out in 1996.

item.title: Best-selling The Housemaid author Freida McFadden reveals true identity
item.description: One of the biggest mysteries in publishing has been solved, after the best-selling US author known to millions of readers as Freida McFadden revealed her true identity.

item.title: Live: Festival of footy kicks off with Crows–Blues sellout in Adelaide
item.description: The fourth edition of AFL's Gather Round gets underway with the Adelaide Crows hosting Carlton at a packed Adelaide Oval.

item.title: Big Mistakes review – Schitt’s Creek creator Dan Levy excels in new cringe comedy
item.description: There are, broadly speaking, two types of television shows: the ones that make stars and the ones made by stars.

item.title: Beloved Queensland pub faces demolition for resort-style development
item.description: A historic pub in Mackay will be demolished if an application for a resort-style development on the site is approved.

item.title: You Are the Führer’s Unrequited Love by Jean-Noël Orengo review – Hitler, Speer and beyond
item.description: In April 1975, Simon Wiesenthal, the Holocaust survivor whose efforts to track down Nazi war criminals had earned him the title of “Nazi hunter”, wrote a letter to Albert Speer, the Nazi war criminal.

item.title: Artemis crew returning to Earth with 'all the good stuff' from Moon discoveries
item.description: The Artemis II crew said they have "many more pictures" and "many more stories" to share with the world as they prepare to return to Earth.

item.title: 'Growing up with deaf parents inspired my new comedy show'
item.description: When Ray Bradshaw was growing up, he never thought there was anything unusual about the fact he could hear while his parents were deaf.

item.title: Artemis II is 'inspiring' a whole generation
item.description: Susan Charlesworth has a rather rare and unique job that involves preparing people for something very few humans have ever expierenced - travelling into space.

item.title: Tigers searching for third straight win to end eight-year streak
item.description: The Wests Tigers themselves might not be talking about it, but every sicko you know of a black, white and gold persuasion certainly is.

item.title: You, Me & Tuscany review – slick romcom offers solidly charming getaway
item.description: Halle Bailey is Anna, hopelessly navigating life after the death of her mother, torn between the worlds of adult responsibility and inner child whimsy.

item.title: ‘My background cringes me out’: Jack Whitehall on poshness, comedy and his lockdown romance
item.description: ‘After every tour, I hate the sound of my voice,’ the actor and comedian says.

item.title: Hundreds more PSOs at Melbourne stations if Liberals win state election
item.description: The Victorian Opposition is promising to recruit hundreds more protective service officers if it wins the state election in November.

item.title: $10m horse will never race, but owner insists she is worth every cent
item.description: The first filly out of champion mare Winx sold at auction for a world record $10 million in 2024.

item.title: Dan Rath: Help Me Please review – you won’t find another standup with more jokes a minute
item.description: Don’t come to Dan Rath’s new show expecting something coherent.

item.title: Comedian Grace Jarvis on most bizarre encounter working in sex shop
item.description: But look a little closer, and you'll see why Rolling Stone last year dubbed this rising Queensland comic a must-see at the Edinburgh Fringe Festival.

item.title: Early season brilliance puts Gawn on track for record All-Australian nod
item.description: The first month of the 2026 AFL season has come and gone, and the ladder is finally beginning to take shape.

item.title: Barcelona on the brink after red card and Alvarez stunner sparks Atlético win
item.description: When the final whistle went, the man in black disappeared out of sight and set off running up the tunnel.

item.title: 'It's all good': Child sex offender allowed to stay in Australia after visa reinstated
item.description: A British man who sexually abused a nine-year-old girl has had his Australian permanent visa reinstated by a tribunal, after it was automatically cancelled following his conviction.

item.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
item.description: With minutes before a self-imposed deadline – and an unprecedented threat to wipe out Iran's "civilisation" - US President Donald Trump announced that a two-week ceasefire had been agreed to halt the war.

item.title: Egg-laying hens in cages set for better living conditions under new laws
item.description: The Victorian government is introducing new regulations for the care and management of poultry, including changes to chicken cages.

item.title: Family still waits to know how their loved one died, one year on
item.description: The family of Indigenous man Kumanjayi White has criticised the secrecy around the independent review into his death, which has been handed to NT Police and the DPP but not released publicly.

item.title: Paris Saint-Germain v Liverpool: Champions League quarter-final, first leg – live
item.description: double quotation markWhile Arsenal were busy Arsenaling their way to a worthy but dull Bigger Cup quarter-final first leg win at Sporting on Tuesday, the players of Real Madrid and Bayern Munich released the collective handbrake and performed many high-speed donuts, wheel spins and Rockfords as a pleasing counterpoint to the careful mirror-signal-manoeuvring on display at Lisbon’s Estádio José Alvalade.

item.title: British novelist Gwendoline Riley wins a $175k Windham-Campbell prize
item.description: British novelist Gwendoline Riley is among eight writers set to receive $175,000 (£130,000) each in recognition of their life’s work.

item.title: Starmer says UK wants to help with opening Hormuz strait amid closure reports
item.description: The UK has a “job” to help reopen the strait of Hormuz, Keir Starmer has said, as Iranian reports said the key shipping route was closed again just hours after a supposed ceasefire.

item.title: Greetings from downtown Cairo, where unpretentious cafés are part of centuries-old charm
item.description: Far-Flung Postcards is a weekly series in which NPR's international team shares moments from their lives and work around the world.

item.title: New light shed on who benefits most from weight-loss jabs
item.description: People who carry variations in two genes linked to appetite and digestion can lose more weight when taking drugs such as Wegovy and Mounjaro to treat obesity, research suggests.

item.title: An irresistible adventure activity for New Zealand visitors? Delivering the mail by boat
item.description: For a travel destination famous for offering the adrenaline rush of extreme sports, from bungee jumping to the parachute drop, it’s an unlikely tourist activity – but an irresistible one.

item.title: Are OnlyFans models the best way to explain the climate crisis?
item.description: Actor Megan Prescott has joined with Adam McKay in the hope that showing bite-size web videos of women undressing will persuade us to save the world.

item.title: What does the dark side of the moon sound like? Nasa’s sonifications are helping us imagine
item.description: Jaw-dropping dark-siding exploration aside, it’s the mundane details of the Artemis II mission that connect us with the four astronauts slingshotting their way around the moon and back.

item.title: Carnival celebrations at a Hungarian retirement home: János Bődey’s best photograph
item.description: When I saw these two elderly ladies dressed as bride and groom in a retirement home in Páty, near Budapest, my first thought was to wonder why they had chosen those particular costumes.

item.title: Euphoria stars hit red carpet at premiere of third - and possibly final - season
item.description: TV drama Euphoria has been the launchpad for the careers of some of Hollywood's hottest stars - and they have reunited on the red carpet for the premiere of the show's third and possibly final season.

item.title: Two for two? Stella prize winner Evelyn Araluen nominated again for second poetry collection
item.description: Evelyn Araluen has been shortlisted for the Stella prize for her second poetry collection The Rot, four years after she became the first ever poet to win the prize for Australian women and non-binary writers.

item.title: Abortion clinics are closing nationwide. Could urgent care help fill the gap?
item.description: Providing abortions was the last thing Shawn Brown thought she'd be doing when she opened an urgent care clinic in Marquette, a small port town on the remote shores of Michigan's Upper Peninsula.

item.title: 'Made it very attractive': Free rides luring thousands more onto buses
item.description: The offer of a free trip on public transport is turning some Tasmanians into "converts", while others use it for new experiences.

item.title: When is the best time to get your flu vaccine?
item.description: We usually have to wait until winter approaches before we see an increase in cases of influenza, or the flu.

item.title: Books changed my life, says Queen's first reading hero
item.description: Selina Brown pored over books as a child, enthralled at the wonders leaping from the pages.

item.title: Modern heroes and a ravaged Earth: reboot of 1950s space comic Dan Dare has liftoff
item.description: Sufferin’ satellites! The quintessential British space hero Dan Dare is back, 76 years after he first appeared in iconic comic magazine the Eagle.

item.title: Republican wins Georgia runoff election to replace Marjorie Taylor Greene
item.description: Clay Fuller supports the war in Iran.

item.title: Squid sightings spawn hope for fast species recovery in South Australia
item.description: Southern calamari has been spotted in parts of South Australia where it had vanished.

item.title: Arteta admits Arsenal had point to prove after ‘big moment in season’ with win at Sporting
item.description: Mikel Arteta acknowledged that Arsenal had a point to prove against Sporting after losing their last two matches and believes that Kai Havertz’s late winner could prove to be a “big moment” in their season.

item.title: Claims wild-caught Australian seafood will soon 'only be afforded by the rich'
item.description: Australian seafood is set to become more expensive unless fuel supplies return to normal.

item.title: Will Democratic wins affect plans for Florida redistricting?
item.description: After Democrats flipped two seats in surprising legislative elections, Florida political insiders weigh whether Republican should risk redistricting later this month to win more House seats.

item.title: Live: Albanese talks energy security with China's premier
item.description: Anthony Albanese and Chinese Premier Li Qiang have discussed energy security ahead of the prime minister's dash to Singapore later this week.

item.title: Real Madrid 1-2 Bayern Munich: Champions League quarter-final first leg – live reaction
item.description: double quotation mark“This is why you guys come to these games,” Vincent Kompany had said, and here especially.

item.title: Troy the parrot home safe after Dublin Airport adventure
item.description: A parrot discovered at Dublin Airport has been safely reunited with its owner, thanks to the efforts of airport staff and the public.

item.title: Women’s Super League academy teams to play in third tier under new FA proposals
item.description: Major structural changes to the Women’s National League, including four Women’s Super League academy sides entering tier three of the pyramid from 2027 and the introduction of a mid-season split similar to that used in Scotland, have been proposed by the Football Association, the Guardian can reveal.

item.title: Charity cleared after false claims online over migrant welcome project
item.description: A refugee charity subjected to vicious social media attacks over a migrant welcome project in schools has been cleared of wrongdoing after watchdogs found allegations it encouraged pupils to send Valentine’s Day cards to asylum seekers were misleading and false.

item.title: Legendary Disney composer Alan Menken on winning Oscars, Razzies and his ‘filthy’ rock musical
item.description: In early 1991, the composer Alan Menken took a keyboard to St Vincent’s hospital in New York to visit his friend and creative partner, the lyricist Howard Ashman.

item.title: Max Chandler-Mather says Greens can use ‘progressive populism’ to win voters deserting major parties for One Nation
item.description: Max Chandler-Mather says the Greens can use “progressive economic populism” to win over Australians deserting the major parties for One Nation as the firebrand former MP accused the political class of thumbing its nose at the concerns of everyday voters.

item.title: ‘We’ll start a creche’: how the World Surf League is becoming family friendly for parents on tour | Kieran Pender
item.description: This year’s Rip Curl Pro at Bells Beach has felt different for Connor O’Leary.

item.title: 3D heart to let surgeons practise baby's vital heart operation
item.description: A couple say their "world will stop" on the day their baby goes into an operating theatre for life-saving open heart surgery.

item.title: Who needs looksmaxxing when you’ve got Catholicmaxxing? The TikTok trend making religion great again
item.description: What’s with all this maxxing business anyway? Oh, don’t concern yourself too much with the nomenclature.

item.title: Women in Northern Ireland welcome introduction of miscarriage leave
item.description: Northern Ireland has become the first part of the UK where a woman and her partner are entitled to two weeks of paid leave if they experience a miscarriage at any stage of pregnancy.

item.title: Neighbour plan to help food bank expand
item.description: Worcester Foodbank is set to expand after securing permission to take over a neighbouring unit.

item.title: JP Morgan reaches agreement with City airport for Canary Wharf’s tallest tower
item.description: JP Morgan Chase has reached agreement with London City airport to build one of Europe’s tallest office towers in the east of the capital.

item.title: ‘Even more beautiful than I imagined’: the nifty Japanese printing gadget uniting artists worldwide
item.description: Gabriella Marcella felt something in her brain click when she first used a risograph printer.

item.title: How to make better coffee – without spending a fortune
item.description: The Guardian’s journalism is independent.

item.title: Pet Shop Boys review – no hits? No problem on first night of a masterful obscurities run
item.description: ‘Tonight …” says Neil Tennant, with a suave pause, “no hits!” The crowd roars.

item.title: ‘Before I can stop her, my daughter is licking crumbs from the table’: my search for the perfect kids’ menu
item.description: We’re heading out for dinner.

item.title: Intel is going all-in on advanced chip packaging
item.description: Sixteen miles north of Albuquerque, in Rio Rancho, New Mexico, an Intel chip plant sits on more than 200 acres of land.

item.title: The Stranger review – lustrously beautiful and superbly realised modern take on the Camus classic
item.description: A heatstricken reverie of violence and mystery unfolds in this film, a numb ecstasy of the inexplicable, as experienced by a sensitive white European under the unbearable noonday sun.

item.title: Gibson jumps at chance to spearhead Australia against Great Britain
item.description: Talia Gibson says it was an "immediate yes" when asked to be part of Australia's Billie Jean King Cup team against Great Britain.

item.title: The Masters is a welcome oasis in golf’s fractious world, despite its stuffy foibles | Ewan Murray
item.description: It is easy to poke fun at the prissy traditions of the Masters.

item.title: China is winning one AI race, the US another - but either might pull ahead
item.description: In the second half of the 20th Century, it was the race to develop nuclear arms that occupied some of the finest minds in the US and the Soviet Union.

item.title: ‘Italy has the best benefits’: Milan takes on Dubai as home for the super-rich
item.description: Just over a month ago, Dubai was the obvious destination for wealthy Britons in search of a new home.

item.title: Cyclist clinging to reeds, plucked to safety in remote Snowy River rescue
item.description: A cyclist who tried to ride across the Snowy River at Suggan Buggan in remote far-east Gippsland has been plucked to safety in a dramatic overnight rescue.

item.title: Call for parents to teach online privacy like road safety
item.description: Parents should teach children online privacy as an essential life skill in the same way as stranger danger or road safety, the UK's data watchdog has said.

item.title: Wrecked boats spill styrofoam, chemicals into Great Barrier Reef for months
item.description: Boat wrecks still line parts of north Queensland's coast months after Cyclone Koji crossed between Bowen and Ayr.

item.title: Wireless Festival boss defends Kanye West booking and asks people to forgive rapper
item.description: The managing director of Wireless Festival has defended the decision to choose Kanye West as a headliner, encouraging people to offer the rapper "forgiveness".

item.title: Wireless festival says Kayne West still due to perform despite calls for ban
item.description: The promoter of Wireless festival has stood by the decision to have Kanye West perform at the event, despite an outcry over the rapper’s antisemitism and calls to cancel his appearance.

item.title: 'Are you sure?': First Nations voters asked about identity at polling booths
item.description: First Nations voters have told elected officials about facing discrimination at polling booths when attempting to cast their vote in the SA First Nations Voice to Parliament vote.

item.title: Hidden costs mean many comedians make 'zero profit' from festival runs
item.description: How much would you pay for someone to turn a projector on for you every night for a week? $100? $200 and a couple drinks?.

item.title: Live: Artemis II breaks distance record, Moon 'bright spot' named after commander's late wife
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, surpassing the record set by Apollo 13 in 1970 at 400,171 kilometres.

item.title: Ladysmith Black Mambazo's 'wise elder' dies aged 77
item.description: Albert Mazibuko, who sang in iconic South African group Ladysmith Black Mambazo for more than five decades, has died aged 77.

item.title: The FA Cup still has an important place. This weekend was proof
item.description: The soccer calendar has been particularly quirky this year.

item.title: Over-the-counter medication abortion? These researchers say it would be safe
item.description: Imagine that you're pregnant, a few weeks in, and you decide you want an abortion.

item.title: Is a new weight-loss drug making people fall out of love?
item.description: What are peptides, are they safe and is there evidence to back up the hype?.

item.title: Championship, Chelsea v Tottenham in Women’s FA Cup, and more: football – live
item.description: The new Football Weekly has dropped.

item.title: Georgina Hayden’s quick and easy recipe for gochujang butter salmon | Quick and easy
item.description: The classic combination of soy sauce and honey salmon is a staple in our house, and works for kids and adults alike.

item.title: ‘All we can do now is pray they continue’: Maasai welcome the first rains but know that drought is far from over
item.description: Kampalei has lived for more than 50 of his 70 years with his family and animals in Oldonyonyokie, a hamlet in southern Kenya’s Kajiado county.
